title: Prism V3 Meta Schema
description: Extended metadata schema for a simulated user profile in the Prism V3 experiment, including drift classification, vulnerability context, crisis windows, social context, and ethical notes.

user_id.description: Unique identifier for the simulated user.

name.description: Display name of the simulated user.

profile.description: Brief narrative profile describing the user's background and situation.

monthly_income.description: Monthly income value. May be a numeric amount or a descriptive string (e.g. 'variable', 'unknown').

event_day.description: The day index on which the key life event occurs within the simulation window.

event.description: Description of the key life event that drives behavioral drift.

data_range.description: The calendar window covered by the simulated data.

start.description: Start date of the data range (ISO 8601).

end.description: End date of the data range (ISO 8601).

v3_extensions.description: Prism V3-specific extensions for drift analysis, vulnerability assessment, and ethical review.

drift_class.description: Classification of the expected behavioral drift severity.

vulnerability_class.description: Free-text label describing the type of vulnerability modeled (e.g. 'financial_shock', 'grief', 'isolation').

crisis_windows.description: Predicted time windows during which crisis-level signals may emerge.

day_range.description: Inclusive start and end day indices for the crisis window.

trigger.description: The event or condition that triggers the crisis window.

expected_severity.description: Anticipated severity level during this crisis window.

social_context.description: Social and relational context for the simulated user.

living_situation.description: Description of the user's living arrangement.

support_network.description: Qualitative description of the user's support network strength and composition.

key_relationships.description: List of key relationship labels relevant to the simulation (e.g. 'spouse', 'therapist', 'estranged_parent').

ethical_notes.description: Free-text notes on ethical considerations, potential harms, and mitigations for this simulation scenario.
